components.task_instruction: You are a precise text classifier. Read the input text and assign it, based solely on its meaning and language, to exactly one of the categories defined below, being especially careful to distinguish between regular and extreme sentiment.
components.label::positive: Favorable, approving, or clearly complimentary statements that indicate something is good, enjoyable, or successful, but without intense excitement or overwhelming praise. If the text is positive but not especially enthusiastic or passionate, select "positive" instead of "very positive".
components.label::very positive: Extremely enthusiastic, glowing, or unambiguously effusive statements showing strong praise or delight. Choose "very positive" (not "positive") if the language expresses unusually high admiration, excitement, or heartfelt affection, beyond simple acknowledgment of good qualities.
components.boundary_rules:
- If the text expresses a negative sentiment but without strong intensity, persistent complaint, or harsh language, choose "negative". If the negativity is severe, deeply critical, or uses intense or repeated negative language, choose "very negative".
- If the text is generally factual, indifferent, non-committal, or only mildly notes flaws without explicit negative evaluation, choose "neutral" rather than "negative".
- If the text is clearly favorable but the positive tone is moderate, restrained, or lacks strong emotional expression, choose "positive". Select "very positive" only when the text is unmistakably enthusiastic, affectionate, exuberant, or uses superlatives/extreme praise.
